region_of_origin: Middle East / South Asia (served in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general kebab food product class; no specific vegetable kebab platter class exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for vegetable kebab platter. A0CKM (mixed cooked vegetables) used as closest defensible anchor given the primary vegetable composition.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for vegetable kebab platter dish.

allergens: may contain gluten if breadcrumbs used as binder

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard vegetable kebab platter serving is approximately 250g, comprising four to six individual kebab pieces with accompanying chutneys and salad garnish.

nutrient_source.title: Veg Kebab Platter — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Veg Kebab Platter (cr_id G00010380) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous mixed vegetable kebab entry (SR Legacy #21362 and component analysis). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and detailed micronutrients not reported by HPB; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed vegetable patty / legume-vegetable kebab
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for vegetable kebab platter. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous mixed legume-vegetable patty entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (chickpea-based dishes GI ~28–40; mixed vegetable preparations GI ~30–40). Carbohydrate per serving = 13.1g (5.24g/100g × 250g); GL = 35 × 13.1 / 100 = 4.6 (low). Estimation is conservative and defensible given the low carbohydrate content and legume/vegetable base.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; sodium at 1080mg/100g represents approximately 54% of the 2000mg daily sodium target in a single 100g portion, and a standard 250g platter delivers ~2700mg sodium — exceeding the full daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low GI (~35) are favourable, but high fat (26.4g/100g) and high sodium (1080mg/100g) require caution
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and estimated low GI make this dish broadly suitable for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high fat content (26.4g/100g) may impair post-meal insulin sensitivity, and the very high sodium load poses a secondary cardiovascular risk that is elevated in people with Type 2 Diabetes — request reduced-salt preparation and avoid pairing with high-GI flatbread or sweet chutneys.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g and total fat 26.4g/100g with cholesterol 85mg/100g present compounded cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is not recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease in its standard preparation due to extreme sodium (1080mg/100g) and elevated fat and cholesterol; if consumed, portion size should be strictly limited to 50–75g and preparation must use significantly reduced salt and minimal added oil.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g exceeds the 400mg/100g RA threshold; nightshade vegetables (potato, green chili, tomato) may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in standard preparation due to extreme sodium and nightshade content; turmeric and spinach provide anti-inflammatory benefits but are insufficient to offset the sodium and solanine exposure across the platter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetable Kebab Platter is a grilled vegetable-based dish popular in Middle Eastern and South Asian cuisines, widely served in Singapore's restaurant and hawker settings. The platter typically comprises four to six individual kebab pieces made from a spiced mixture of mashed or chopped vegetables — commonly potato, spinach, onion, capsicum, chickpeas or lentils — bound together and grilled or pan-fried on skewers or as patties. The kebabs are seasoned with turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, and green chili, giving them a warm, aromatic, mildly spiced flavour profile with a slightly crisp exterior and soft interior. The platter is typically served with mint chutney, tamarind sauce, sliced onion rings, and lemon wedges. Nutritionally, the dish is low in carbohydrates (5.24g/100g) and has an estimated low glycaemic index (~35), making it broadly suitable for glycaemic management. However, it is high in fat (26.4g/100g) and carries an extremely high sodium content (1080mg/100g), which is a significant concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The dish provides moderate protein (10.7g/100g), useful calcium (118mg/100g), and beneficial potassium (387mg/100g). It is vegetarian, vegan-compatible, and halal. Clinically, the sodium level is the dominant concern and limits suitability for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients unless prepared with significantly reduced salt.

Is veg kebab platter high in sodium?
Can I eat veg kebab with high blood pressure?
What is in a vegetable kebab platter?
Veg kebab platter nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010380, confidence 0.95, provenance canonical, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA analogous entry. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous legume-vegetable entries. All mandatory multilingual names present. All hard gates pass.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (0.0g/100g reported). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this vegetable kebab platter are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish as of 2026. Saturated fat (4.2g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous mixed vegetable kebab/patty composition given the oil-based grilling method and vegetable-legume base. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database chickpea and mixed vegetable preparation entries (GI range 28–40); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. The dish is classified under 'Meat and alternatives' in the HPB source despite being fully vegetarian, reflecting the dish's role as a protein-contributing main course alternative. Nightshade vegetables (potato, tomato, green chili) are common ingredients in this platter and may be relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis sensitivity.
